description: Machine-readable runtime dependency allowlist for Handshake product code. Declares the only allowed classes of external runtime inputs (operator-provided model/tensor/CUI-portable artifacts, all operator-gated) and the forbidden runtime dependency classes (outside apps, external daemons, Docker-default paths, SQLite, Redis/Valkey, CDN-loaded runtime assets). Consumed by: app/src/lib/dependency_policy/allowlist.ts (typed accessor), app/scripts/lib/dependency_policy_scans.mjs (tripwire scanners), app/scripts/check-dependency-policy.mjs (validator hook, MT-032), app/scripts/generate-third-party-notices.mjs (MT-029), and src/backend/handshake_core/src/dependency_policy/mod.rs (Rust counterpart parity test). This file is product code, not governance: it gates what the shipped product may depend on at runtime.

allowed_external_runtime_inputs.description: Operator-provided GGUF model files loaded by the model runtime. Data artifacts only: never runtime authority, storage authority, or proof shortcuts (MT-023).
Operator-provided safetensors model weights loaded by the model runtime. Data artifacts only (MT-023).
Operator-provided tensor data artifacts (steering vectors, embeddings, adapters). Data artifacts only (MT-023).

CUI portable artifacts (operator-provided portable tool bundles). Allowed only where a separate owning WP explicitly gates them; the gate defaults closed (MT-022). WP-009 core editor/index/memory behavior must never depend on this class.

forbidden_runtime_dependency_classes.description: External desktop applications required for core product behavior. Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native.
Externally managed servers or daemons (Redis, Valkey, Memcached, external brokers) as runtime or cache authority. PostgreSQL+EventLedger managed by Handshake is the only durable-state authority.
Docker or containers required by default (non-opt-in) product paths. Container tooling is allowed only behind the explicitly opt-in sandbox isolation adapters listed in docker_opt_in_exceptions.
SQLite in any form (direct, transitive, wasm, node:sqlite) is forbidden in product code. PostgreSQL+EventLedger is canonical for durable state.

Runtime-loaded assets from CDNs. All editor/UI assets (Monaco, Tiptap, fonts, workers) must be bundled, lockfile-governed, and offline-capable. @monaco-editor/loader and @monaco-editor/react are forbidden because their default loader fetches Monaco from a CDN.

bundled_libraries.reason: Rich-text editor core and extensions for Loom documents (WP-009). Bundled, lockfile-governed product library.
Tiptap's underlying editor model and its small MIT helper packages.
Code editor surface. Bundled via the monaco-editor npm package with locally bundled Vite workers; the CDN-based @monaco-editor/loader is forbidden.
CRDT collaboration model and its MIT runtime helpers.
Statically compiled source parsers for AI-ready-data chunking. Grammars are compiled into the binary; no runtime .so/.wasm grammar downloads (MT-028).

docker_opt_in_exceptions.reason: Opt-in HardIsolation sandbox adapter. The default sandbox adapter is WSL2 podman (sandbox/bootstrap.rs); DockerAdapter::try_new is failure-tolerant, so every core product path works without Docker installed.

built_output_scan_exceptions.reason: Excalidraw 0.18 hardcodes a static ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL constant pointing at esm.sh inside its published bundle; the string cannot be removed without patching the library. The actual minified shape is `Y(yW,"ASSETS_FALLBACK_URL",`https://esm.sh/...`)`, i.e. the marker sits ~30 chars BEFORE the esm.sh literal, so the exception window is forward-only (marker must precede the hit) and capped at max_marker_distance=64 — far tighter than the wider proximity window an attacker could exploit by planting a second hostile esm.sh import within range. Its core fonts are bundled by Vite into app/dist/assets (*.woff2 emitted at build), so the fallback stays dormant in the packaged product. HARDENED (H1, external-validator finding): the built-output scan now also asserts max_total_occurrences=1 across the whole product dist tree — if esm.sh appears more than once anywhere in built output the scan FAILS regardless of marker proximity, so a hostile second occurrence cannot be smuggled in beside the legitimate one. Tracked risk for the canvas-owning WP: an Excalidraw surface relying on lazily fetched non-bundled assets would make this fallback live — that surface must pin window.EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH to a packaged path and prove offline canvas load with esm.sh aborted.

scan_self_exempt_paths.description: HARDENED (H3, external-validator finding): replaces a blanket excludePathSubstrings:['dependency_policy','dependency-policy'] exemption that was so broad it silently excluded the REAL product runtime file app/src/harness/dependency_policy_harness.tsx (and any other product file whose path merely contains the substring) from the CDN + docker source tripwires. Only the EXACT policy-data, scanner, and test files below — which legitimately embed forbidden-pattern literals as data or negative-test fixtures — are exempt from those source scans. Every other product source file (including dependency_policy_harness.tsx) is scanned like any other product source. Paths are repo-relative and matched EXACTLY (no substring widening).

source_tripwire_exceptions.description: Pattern-scoped source tripwire exceptions for existing owned product code that intentionally exposes local operator-configured model runtime URLs. Exceptions are exact path + forbidden class + source pattern; they must not be used for new proof defaults, storage/cache authority, desktop-app dependencies, or dev-server instructions.

entries.reason: Existing local LLM registry support resolves an operator-configured Ollama endpoint; this exception does not allow new unmanaged proof defaults or dev-server dependencies.
Existing Ollama runtime adapter documents and tests its local endpoint; this exception is exact to the adapter and does not authorize new server-daemon defaults elsewhere.
Existing model runtime invariant tests cover local Ollama configuration; this exception is exact and does not permit new unmanaged proof or cache authority.
Sandbox network comments use a local model-service loopback example while documenting opt-in loopback bridging, not a default external daemon dependency.
Docker bridge comments use a local model-service loopback example while documenting opt-in loopback allowlisting, not a default external daemon dependency.
Managed PostgreSQL module states the negative invariant that no SQLite fallback is used; this is not a fallback implementation.
Storage tests contain negative assertions against legacy SQLite references; exact exception keeps the production tripwire active elsewhere.
Diagnostics bundle tests use SQLite-shaped fixture data to prove local-authority redaction and validation, not to configure storage.
Atelier path validation rejects SQLite-shaped storage inputs; exact exception covers negative validation logic only.

docker_artifact_scan.description: HARDENED (H2, external-validator finding): the code-source tripwire (scan_self code extensions) excludes .yml/.yaml/Dockerfile/Containerfile/.sh, so a docker-compose.dev.yml or Dockerfile dropped into a product scan root would not trip MT-024. This walker scans product_scan_roots for docker-orchestration artifact files independently of the code-source walker. Any matching file FAILS MT-024 unless its repo-relative path is under one of the docker_opt_in_exceptions path_prefixes (the opt-in HardIsolation sandbox adapter ONLY). Shell scripts match only when their content references docker run / docker compose / docker-compose, so unrelated .sh scripts are not false-positived.
